strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: A five-answer bank paired with the validated three-reviewer falsification mechanism may retain much of the champion's accuracy at eight calls per case rather than twelve; earlier five-solver repair systems reached 0.417–0.500 pooled accuracy.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Because the five-bank winner matched its candidate oracle without creating any new correct repairs, three falsifying reviewers that must choose from the same shuffled bank may preserve its corrections while reducing unsupported answer generation.
strategies.name: Five-Bank Falsifying Selection

strategies.hypothesis: Changing only the champion's trigger can determine whether its gains are concentrated in genuinely unresolved banks; reviewing only without a strict base majority may retain most corrections while avoiding unnecessary calls on confident cases.
strategies.name: Majority-Gated Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: The prior committee system corrected two base errors without harmful reversals. Giving the full-bank plurality one delegate vote alongside the three comparison reviewers may preserve strong global consensus when committee delegates or reviewers fragment.
strategies.name: Committee Delegate Fusion
